name: NetKet (Neural Network Quantum States)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Giuseppe Carleo et al.

performance.notes: JAX-based; GPU/TPU accelerated; Monte Carlo sampling is compute-intensive

agent_readiness.notes: Neural network quantum states (NQS) for many-body quantum systems. import netket as nk. graph = nk.graph.Hypercube(length=10, n_dim=1, pbc=True). hi = nk.hilbert.Spin(s=0.5, N=graph.n_nodes). H = nk.operator.Ising(hi, graph, h=1.0). model = nk.models.RBM(alpha=1). sampler = nk.sampler.MetropolisLocal(hi, n_chains=16). vstate = nk.vqs.MCState(sampler, model, n_samples=1008). optimizer = nk.optimizer.Sgd(learning_rate=0.01). sr = nk.optimizer.SR(diag_shift=0.1). gs = nk.driver.VMC(H, optimizer, variational_state=vstate, preconditioner=sr). gs.run(n_iter=300).

gotchas: Extremely domain-specific — quantum many-body physics research only
Requires condensed matter physics and quantum computing background
VMC + SR optimization is sensitive to hyperparameters
Small community — limited Stack Overflow support
best_when: Neural network quantum states for ground state energy estimation
Variational Monte Carlo (VMC) for quantum spin systems
Research on quantum many-body physics with ML
When DMRG or exact diagonalization is intractable
avoid_when: Quantum circuit simulation (use Qiskit or Cirq)
Machine learning without quantum physics context
summary: NetKet implements neural network quantum states (NQS) for many-body quantum physics. nk.models.RBM + MCState + VMC driver. Variational Monte Carlo with stochastic reconfiguration (SR). JAX-backed GPU acceleration. Apache-2.0 by Giuseppe Carleo. Extremely specialized — quantum condensed matter physics research only. For spin systems, Ising models, and ground state estimation. Not for quantum circuit simulation.
